page: Microsoft Azure E20pds_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the EPDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 20 dedicated physical cores powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor operating at 3.0 GHz. The CPU architecture is arm64, with a cache hierarchy consisting of 64 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. The server is equipped with 160.0 GB of RAM, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It includes 768 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, with no integrated GPUs.

Performance benchmarks place the E20pds_v5 in the average tier for most operations. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score is 1822.88, while the multi-core score reaches 36030.32. Memory bandwidth is measured at 1405.13 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, and 150.41 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads, with large-block writes at 73.73 GB/sec. In application-specific tests, the server achieves a PassMark database operations score of 13959.39 and handles 28.38 million Redis SET operations per second. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression is a relative weak point at 8.7 GB/sec, though multi-threaded decompression scales to an average-tier 73.66 GB/sec.

Given its 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio and 768 GB of local storage, the E20pds_v5 is structured for memory-intensive workloads, database management, and web serving. It delivers 24.42 GB/sec of static web serving throughput and handles small-model LLM inference at 145.94 tokens/sec for text generation. However, it exhibits weaker performance on medium-model prompt processing at 23.64 tokens/sec. Qualitatively, the ARM-based architecture provides a resource-dense configuration with dedicated physical cores, offering a cost-efficient alternative to traditional x86 architectures for memory-heavy and database-driven applications that do not require specialized AVX or AMX instruction sets.
description: Microsoft Azure E20pds_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the EPDSv5 family, featuring 20 dedicated physical cores on a 3.0 GHz Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor. It is equipped with 160.0 GB of RAM, offering a memory-optimized 8.0 GB per core ratio, alongside 768 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this arm64 server delivers balanced, average-tier performance across key benchmarks, including a PassMark CPU score of 14045.7 and a database operations score of 13959.39. It handles 28.38 million Redis SET operations per second and provides 24.42 GB/sec of static web serving throughput. Lacking AVX and AMX instruction sets, it is not designed for heavy vector math but serves as a cost-efficient, resource-dense platform for memory-intensive workloads, database hosting, and web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E20pds_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 20 dedicated physical cores, 160.0 GB of RAM, and 768 GB of local storage, optimized for memory-intensive database and web serving workloads.

tagline: An ARM-powered, memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated physical cores and local storage for database and web serving workloads.

160.0 GB of RAM providing a memory-optimized 8.0 GB per core ratio
768 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Average-tier database performance with 13,959.39 PassMark database score and 28.38M Redis SET ops/sec
